mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or if the strategy Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, persistent monetary accommodation compresses the equity discount rate and suppresses risk-free alternatives, supporting positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If triggered periods produce negative average 60-day returns or a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm a coordinated demand-supply recovery, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns that exceed either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce higher mean 20-day returns than the sum of individual single-signal effects (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery regimes, month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate signal rapid labor market repair and restored household income, predicting positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines during post-shock recovery months coincide with negative 5-day returns more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month jumps in housing starts exceeding 50 thousand units signal accelerating construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, predicting positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 50 thousand units month-over-month
failure_condition: If such large increases in HOUST are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate genuine labor market tightening rather than participation-driven noise, forecasting positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces negative average 60-day returns or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in the consumer price index above 0.5% signal emergent inflation pressure that reduces real earnings and prompts monetary tightening fears, predicting negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL increases greater than 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion regimes, two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an impending earnings recession, predicting negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If two-month declines in INDPRO during late-cycle expansions coincide with positive 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.
